source_pr.title: *: fix unstable test for lfu and index merge
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/45649

close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/45632

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: The LFU cache test asserted memory cost immediately after `Put(...)`, but internal updates could be asynchronous; assertions could run before the cost accounting stabilized. Separately, an index merge test target sometimes needed a longer timeout (executor/test/indexmergereadtest, statistics/handle/cache/internal/lfu/lfu_cache_test.go).
failure_signature: Intermittent cost assertion mismatch in LFU cache tests due to async updates; and occasional timeouts in index merge tests under a too-short timeout setting.
root_cause_explanation: The instability is timing-related: LFU cache cost accounting can lag behind `Put(...)` operations, so checking `Cost()` immediately can be flaky. The fix adds explicit waits (`lfu.wait()`) between updates and assertions so the internal state converges before checking. In addition, the index merge test target's timeout is increased from short to moderate to reduce timeout flakes (statistics/handle/cache/internal/lfu/lfu_cache_test.go, executor/test/indexmergereadtest/BUILD.bazel).
fix_pattern: Add explicit synchronization/waiting before asserting async-updated state, and increase too-short test timeouts when needed.

evidence: evidence: *: fix unstable test for lfu and index merge
